items.label: Project jurisdiction
items.hint: Selects which standards family the skill loads. GB = BS 7671; EU/INT = IEC 60364; US = NFPA 70.

items.label: Earthing system
items.hint: TN-C-S = combined N+PE upstream of service, separated downstream (most UK/PME). TT = consumer electrode only (rural / no PME).

items.label: Supply voltage (V phase-to-phase for 3-phase, phase-to-neutral for single)
Declared Ze — supply network earth-fault impedance (Ω)
items.hint: From DNO declaration or assumed per jurisdiction default (UK TN-C-S typical 0.35Ω; TT 21Ω+).

items.label: Building has a lightning protection system (LPS)?
items.hint: Per BS EN 62305 / NFPA 780. Affects earthing electrode bonding requirements.

items.label: Extraneous-conductive-parts requiring main protective bonding
items.hint: List of services / structures requiring bonding to MET. Each entry: { type, location_descriptor }.

items.label: Site soil/ground conditions
items.hint: Drives soil resistivity assumption for TT electrode sizing.

items.label: Number of earth electrodes planned
items.hint: TT typically 1-4 rods; TN-C-S supplementary electrodes optional.

items.label: Target earth electrode resistance Ra (Ω)
items.hint: GB TT typical ≤200Ω; US NEC ≤25Ω or use 2 electrodes; EU/INT per IEC 60364-5-54.

items.label: Distribution boards on this project (from db-layout intent if present)
items.hint: Optional — if db-layout intent is supplied via cross_drawing_context, this is auto-populated.

items.label: Main earthing terminal (MET) location
items.hint: Room / area description. E.g. 'plant room', 'meter cupboard'.

items.label: Locations requiring supplementary equipotential bonding
items.hint: Bathroom (BS 7671 Section 701 / IEC 60364-7-701), pool (Section 702 / 60364-7-702 / NEC 680.26), medical Group 2 (Section 710 / 60364-7-710 / NEC 517).

items.label: Assets bonded as part of life-safety system
items.hint: Fire pump, emergency generator, smoke extract fans, etc. Will appear in produced earthing intent for downstream consumers.

items.label: Default RCD type for circuits requiring earth-leakage protection
items.hint: Type A = AC + pulsating DC (covers most modern equipment). Type B = handles DC components (required for EV chargers, PV, some VFDs).
